children.name: Rana muscosa
children.description: The southern mountain yellow-legged frog, formerly known as the mountain yellow-legged frog, is a species of true frog endemic to California in the United States. It occurs in the San Jacinto Mountains, San Bernardino Mountains, and San Gabriel Mountains in Southern California and the Southern Sierra Nevada. It is a federally listed endangered species, separated into two distinct population segments (DPS): a northern DPS, listed endangered in 2014, and a southern DPS that was listed endangered in 2002.

children.name: Lithobates
children.description: Lithobates — a genus of true frogs in the family Ranidae.

children.name: Lithobates areolatus
children.description: The crawfish frog is a medium-sized species of frog native to the prairies and grasslands of the central United States. It gets its name because it inhabits the burrows of crayfish for most of the year. They have defined golden or black circles all over their body.

children.name: Lithobates brownorum
children.description: Lithobates brownorum, commonly known as Browns' leopard frog, is a species of frog native to southern Veracruz and northeastern Oaxaca east through the Yucatan Peninsula and the uplands of Chiapas in southern Mexico through Guatemala and Honduras to Nicaragua.

children.name: Lithobates capito
children.description: The gopher frog is a species of frog in the family Ranidae, endemic to the southeastern United States. It primarily inhabits the threatened sandhill communities, flatwoods, and scrub in the Atlantic coastal plain, where it is usually found near ponds.

children.name: Lithobates chichicuahutla
children.description: The Lago de las Minas frog is a species of frog in the family Ranidae. It is endemic to the Las Minas Lake in the Oriental Basin of Puebla, Mexico. Its natural habitat is vegetation surrounding the lake.

children.name: Lithobates clamitans
children.description: {{Speciesbox | name = Green frog | image = Male Green Frog - Hunterdon County, NJ.jpg | image_caption = Male, Tewksbury Township, New Jersey | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = G5 | status2_system = TNC | status2_ref = | genus = Lithobates | species = clamitans | authority = (Latreille, 1801) | range_map = Ran_clam_NA_range.gif | range_map_caption = Green frog range | synonyms = *Rana horiconensis *Rana fontinalis *Rana clamitans | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text L.

children.name: Lithobates heckscheri
children.description: The river frog (Lithobates heckscheri) is a species of aquatic frog in the family Ranidae.

children.name: Lithobates lenca
children.description: The Lenca leopard frog (Lithobates lenca) is a species of true frog found in the Chortis Highlands of southwestern Honduras at altitudes of 1560 to 2080 m.

children.name: Lithobates megapoda
children.description: The big-footed leopard frog or bigfoot leopard frog (Lithobates megapoda, formerly Rana megapoda), is a species of frog in the family Ranidae endemic to western central Mexico where it is found in the Nayarit, Jalisco, Michoacán, and Guanajuato states.

children.name: Lithobates neovolcanicus
children.description: #REDIRECT Transverse volcanic leopard frog.

children.name: Lithobates onca
children.description: The relict leopard frog is a species of frog in the family Ranidae, endemic to the United States.

children.name: Lithobates sevosus
children.description: The Mississippi gopher frog, also known commonly as the dark gopher frog, the dusky gopher frog, and the St. Tammany gopher frog, is a critically endangered species of frog in the family Ranidae. The species is endemic to the southern United States.

children.name: Lithobates sierrae
children.description: Lithobates sierrae — a species in the genus Lithobates, family Ranidae. Also known as true frogs.

children.name: Lithobates vibicarius
children.description: Lithobates vibicarius, commonly known as either green-eyed frog (after its green eyes).

children.name: Odorrana andersonii

children.name: Odorrana anlungensis
children.description: Odorrana anlungensis is a species of frog in the family Ranidae that is endemic to China: it is only known from its type locality, Mount Longtou in Anlong County, Guizhou.

children.name: Odorrana aureola
children.description: Odorrana aureola, also known as the Phu Luang cliff frog or gold-flanked odorous frog ( ), is a true frog species from northeastern Thailand.

children.description: Odorrana chapaensis (Vietnam sucker frog or Chapa torrent frog) is a species of frog in the family Ranidae that is found in southern Yunnan in China and in northern Vietnam.

children.name: Odorrana chloronota
children.description: Odorrana chloronota, commonly known as the chloronate huia frog or copper-cheeked frog, is a species of frog in the family Ranidae that is found in Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, Vietnam, and possibly Bangladesh and Nepal.

children.description: Odorrana exiliversabilis (Fujian bamboo-leaf frog) is a species of frogs in the family Ranidae that is endemic to southeastern China where it is found in Fujian, western Zhejiang, and southern Anhui provinces.

children.name: Odorrana fengkaiensis

children.name: Glandirana rugosa
children.description: The Japanese wrinkled frog is a species of true frog native to Japan and introduced to Hawaii in the late 19th century. It has sometimes been regarded as a single species with the Imienpo Station frog which is found on the East Asian mainland. The two species are distinguished from others by their rough and uneven skin.

children.name: Indosylvirana
children.description: Indosylvirana — a genus of true frogs in the family Ranidae.

children.name: Indosylvirana aurantiaca
children.description: Indosylvirana aurantiaca, commonly known as the golden frog, is a species of frog endemic to the Western Ghats of India.

children.name: Indosylvirana caesari
children.description: Indosylvirana caesari — a species in the genus Indosylvirana, family Ranidae. Also known as true frogs.

children.name: Indosylvirana doni

children.name: Indosylvirana flavescens

children.name: Indosylvirana indica
children.description: Indosylvirana indica, the Indian golden-backed frog, is a species of frog in the family Ranidae.

children.name: Indosylvirana intermedia
children.description: Indosylvirana intermedia, or Rao's intermediate golden-backed frog, is a species of ranid frog endemic to southern India.

children.name: Indosylvirana magna
children.description: Indosylvirana magna — a species in the genus Indosylvirana, family Ranidae. Also known as true frogs.

children.name: Indosylvirana montana
children.description: Indosylvirana montana — a species in the genus Indosylvirana, family Ranidae. Also known as true frogs.

children.name: Indosylvirana serendipi
children.description: Indosylvirana serendipi, or the Sri Lankan golden-backed frog, is a species of frog in the family Ranidae.

children.name: Indosylvirana sreeni
children.description: Indosylvirana sreeni, also known as Sreeni's golden-backed frog, is a species of frog in the family Ranidae found in Southern Western and Eastern Ghats in the states of Kerala and Tamil Nadu, India, at elevations of 100 to 1500 meters.

children.name: Indosylvirana temporalis
children.description: Indosylvirana temporalis, commonly known as the bronzed frog or Günther's golden-backed frog, is a species of true frog found in the riparian evergreen forests of the highlands of southwestern Sri Lanka.

children.name: Indosylvirana urbis
children.description: Indosylvirana urbis — a species in the genus Indosylvirana, family Ranidae. Also known as true frogs.
